title: The Opioid Crisis Was Built on a Science Gap. Researchers Are Finally Closing It.
description: For decades, opioids dominated pain medicine not because they were the best option, but because nothing better existed. That just changed. A new class of analgesics — including the first genuinely novel pain mechanism approved by the FDA in over 20 years — targets the peripheral nervous system's pain circuitry directly, leaving addiction pathways untouched. Here's what the science actually shows, what it doesn't, and why access may become the next crisis.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Opioid Crisis Was Built on a Science Gap. Researchers Are Finally Closing It.
